title: achillea wilhelmsii subsp. santolinoides
intro: Achillea wilhelmsii subsp. santolinoides, also known as Yarrow or White Yarrow, is a perennial flowering plant native to China and parts of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Achillea wilhelmsii subsp. santolinoides is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to promote healing and prevent infection.
